r_relatable_problem.primary_problem: Five cycles converge October 2015 → Government debt collapse 2016-2020 → Four fortune opportunities
relatability_triggers: In October 2015... for the first time since 1929... five massively powerful financial cycles united, forming a 'Supercycle' with enormous destructive power.

s_specific_solution.core_mechanism: The Supercycle / The Great Convergence: Five powerful economic cycles discovered by Edward R. Dewey (President Hoover's chief economist) converged in October 2015 for the first time since 1929. These cycles are: (1) Kondratieff Wave - signals weaker economy, soaring unemployment, skyrocketing interest rates, massive debt defaults; (2) Juglar Cycle (7-11 years) - signals massive cash hoarding by businesses, plunging re-investment, job destruction, comatose economy; (3) Kitchin Cycle (40 months) - signals slower business formation, weak consumer demand, slower inventory turnover, chronic unemployment; (4) 20-year economic cycle - signals depression and catastrophe; (5) 60-year economic cycle - signals depression and catastrophe. When these five converge, they create a Supercycle with enormous destructive power. Last convergence: 1929 Great Depression. This convergence triggers four distinct phases: Europe collapse 2015-16, Japan collapse 2016, flight capital rally in US assets 2015-17, USA collapse 2017-2020.
mechanism_proof_points: Larry Edelson - 38-year career, accurately predicted every major twist in gold market since late 1970s
Edward R. Dewey - President Hoover's chief economist who discovered cycles in 1932
Foundation for the Study of Cycles - supported by Harvard, Yale, Princeton, Oxford, Carnegie Institution, Fidelity, AON Insurance
Edelson's perfect calls: 1987 crash, 1999 gold bottom (533% rally), Sept 2011 gold top, March 2009 stock bottom (252% rally), 2014 dollar rally, 2014-15 oil/grain crash
Cycles predicted Great Depression in advance
Cycles predicted 2008 crash (Edelson's chart showing predicted vs actual match)

lead.full_text: This is your moment of truth. What you do in the next few minutes, hours and days could determine your financial destiny for the rest of your life. In October 2015... for the first time since 1929... five massively powerful financial cycles united, forming a 'Supercycle' with enormous destructive power. The last time these cycles converged - nearly nine decades ago - the world was plunged into a Great Depression that lasted more than a decade. This time around, they will trigger the end of one major epoch in human history... and the beginning of a terrifying (and enormously profitable) new one... The age we have all known all our lives - an era in which governments amassed $275 trillion in debts and obligations - is ending. And a new era - the age in which all of us pay the price for our leaders' reckless spending schemes and the obscene debts - is beginning...
lead.hook_sentence: In October 2015... for the first time since 1929... five massively powerful financial cycles united, forming a 'Supercycle' with enormous destructive power.
lead.transition_to_body: Now, before you dismiss Edelson as some kind of extremist, keep this in mind: His forecasts have been amazingly accurate...

three_part_structure.what_they_think_problem_is: Governments have some debt issues and need to cut spending eventually, but the system will muddle through like it always has
three_part_structure.what_real_problem_is: Five massively powerful economic cycles (Kondratieff Wave, Juglar Cycle, Kitchin Cycle, 20-year and 60-year cycles) have converged for the first time since 1929. These cycles govern the rise and fall of economies with mathematical precision. When they last converged, the Great Depression happened. This time they're signaling that the $275 trillion in government debt (Europe, Japan, USA) has reached the tipping point where investors will revolt and stop lending. Europe is already collapsing (22 of 28 EU states deeper in debt despite bailouts, Greece 77% more debt than GDP). Japan has 1 quadrillion yen debt (2.5x their economy). USA has $120 trillion total obligations (6x economy size). When investors stop lending, governments literally run out of money.
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: Politicians chose debt solutions over real solutions. Europe printed money but debt piled up faster. Japan printed 10.3 trillion yen in Jan 2013, another 3.5 trillion in Dec 2014, yet fell into fifth recession since 2000. The Fed printed $1.6 trillion but economy is sinking and revenues falling again. Governments everywhere are borrowing every euro, yen, and dollar investors will loan them - but investors are beginning to revolt (Spanish, Italian, Portuguese bonds cratered June 2015 while US treasuries surged). The debt is so massive it can never be repaid. Investors realize this and the stampede to exit has begun.

Everyone knows Washington will never make a dent in that debt. What most economists know but won't say is that Washington won't be able to even service that much debt for much longer.
villain_profile.secondary_villain: Central banks (ECB, Bank of Japan, Federal Reserve) for money printing that made debt worse

mechanism.explanation_text: Five powerful economic cycles discovered by Edward R. Dewey (President Hoover's chief economist) converged in October 2015 for the first time since 1929. These cycles are: (1) Kondratieff Wave - signals weaker economy, soaring unemployment, skyrocketing interest rates, massive debt defaults; (2) Juglar Cycle (7-11 years) - signals massive cash hoarding by businesses, plunging re-investment, job destruction, comatose economy; (3) Kitchin Cycle (40 months) - signals slower business formation, weak consumer demand, slower inventory turnover, chronic unemployment; (4) 20-year economic cycle - signals depression and catastrophe; (5) 60-year economic cycle - signals depression and catastrophe. When these five converge, they create a Supercycle with enormous destructive power. Last convergence: 1929 Great Depression. This convergence triggers four distinct phases: Europe collapse 2015-16, Japan collapse 2016, flight capital rally in US assets 2015-17, USA collapse 2017-2020.

specimens.quote: Mr. Edelson is one of the few analysts alive who has accurately predicted every major twist and turn in the gold market since the late 1970s. The last time he predicted rising gold prices - in late 1999 - the yellow metal rallied 533% to over $1,900 per ounce. That would be enough to multiply your money more than six times over.

In 1987, Mr. Edelson accurately warned of the largest collapse in U.S. stock market history. And he also predicted that, after the crash, the market would rally to new highs by 1989. The Dow lost nearly 23% of its value in a single day - on October 19, 1987, just as Larry predicted. Then, it rallied nearly 73% to a new high on October 9, 1989, just as Edelson had predicted.

In March of 2009 - while other analysts were still licking their wounds and terrified to even touch a stock - I announced that the worst was over; that stocks were about to catch fire again. RESULT: Since that forecast, the S&P 500 is up a whopping 170%; enough to turn every $10,000 invested into $18,000. And if you had used my forecast to invest in the 3x S&P 500 ETF, you could be up 510% - enough to turn every $10,000 you invested into nearly $61,000.

President Herbert Hoover ordered his chief economist, Edward R. Dewey, to determine what caused the Great Depression. What Dewey found was shocking: Very powerful economic cycles that govern the rise and fall of economies, currencies and investment markets... Dewey's ultimate conclusion was a shocker: Anyone who even casually glanced at charts depicting these cycles could have known about the approaching nightmare well in advance. The Great Depression happened because it was TIME for it to happen.

The chart is an example of my own work. It is the product of my cycles research in 2006 and 2007 - work I did well before the U.S. real estate market cracked. The red line is the cycle I was following. The black line is what actually happened. As you can see, the cycle clearly predicted that the U.S. economy would peak in 2007, then suffer a massive crash. The chart also showed quite clearly that the bottom would come in March of 2009.

Europe crisis CONFIRMS what the cycles are telling us: 22 of the 28 EU member states - including the largest states; Spain, France, Italy and the UK - are deeper in debt now than ever before. In Spain and France, it would take nearly all the money generated by their economies in an entire year to equal their national debts. Greece owes 77% more than its economy produces.

Japan's debt is 1 QUADRILLION YEN. That's a 'one' followed by FIFTEEN zeros. Tokyo's debt is nearly two and one-half times the size of the entire Japanese economy and more than DOUBLE the debt load that recently pushed Greece, Ireland and Portugal to the brink of collapse.

Washington is on the hook for more than $120 trillion. That's more than 6 times the size of the entire U.S. economy. A line of 120 trillion dollar bills would reach around the Earth at the equator more than 467,000 times. It would reach all the way to the sun and back more than 62 times.

The data show that, if you had used this strategy from 2001 through the end of 2010, you could have beat the S&P 500 by 10.5 to 1, with an overall return of 467.8%. That's enough to turn $10,000 into $56,780 or $100,000 into $567,800.

Foundation for the Study of Cycles supporters included: Charles Greeley Abbott (Director, Smithsonian Institution), William Cameron Forbes (Chairman, Carnegie Institution), Ned Johnson (Founder, Fidelity Investments), W. Clement Stone (Founder, AON Insurance), U.S. Senator Everett M. Dirksen, U.S. Vice President Charles G. Dawes

fssit_candidates.statement: In October 2015... for the first time since 1929... five massively powerful financial cycles united, forming a 'Supercycle' with enormous destructive power. The last time these cycles converged - nearly nine decades ago - the world was plunged into a Great Depression that lasted more than a decade.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains your entire financial worldview - the sense that something fundamentally wrong is happening isn't paranoia. The same scientific cycles that govern the rise and fall of empires (discovered by President Hoover's chief economist) have converged for the first time in 86 years. The Great Depression happened because of this convergence. It's happening again NOW.

fssit_candidates.statement: The plain truth is, the most powerful forecasting tools I have ever used are virtually screaming that all hell is about to break loose in Europe... then in Japan... and finally right here, in the United States. The same forecasting tools that accurately predicted the Great Depression - and every major economic event since... Are now warning that the most severe financial crisis any of us has ever seen has begun.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Names the pattern you've sensed but couldn't articulate - the unease you've felt since 2008, the distrust of 'recovery' claims, the awareness that debt keeps growing. The most powerful forecasting tools ever discovered (cycles that predicted the Great Depression with precision) are screaming that the crisis has ALREADY BEGUN. You're not pessimistic - you're realistic.

fssit_candidates.statement: You know that the official national debt is more than $19 trillion and doubling every six years. You also know that Washington owes another $101 trillion that it never talks about. And what's worse, some economists say the real number is much higher - well over $200 trillion. You know this explosion of debt is unsustainable: It has to stop somewhere.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Validates everything you've suspected - the debt numbers Washington admits to are already insane ($19 trillion doubling every 6 years), but the REAL debt including unfunded obligations is $101-$200+ trillion. You weren't crazy to think the system was unsustainable. It IS unsustainable. And the cycles confirm it's ending NOW.
